items.title: Obama Center, Nayland Blake, Danielle Mckinney
items.summary: The “Obamalith” is unveiled while the School of the Art Institute of Chicago wages war against its students’ imagination.

items.title: Painter David Hockney, Who Made the Everyday Otherworldly, Dies at 88
items.summary: His work over a prolific six decades ranged from psychologically precise portraits to luminous depictions of California pool sides.

items.title: “I Shot Andy Warhol” Upends the Myth of the Great Man
items.summary: The radical feminist author of “SCUM Manifesto” and the bygone world of Warhol’s Factory come to life in the 1996 film, now restored to 4K.

items.title: 25 Art Shows Reckoning With the US at 250
items.summary: From Indigenous survivance to quilting to modernism, these exhibitions and projects reframe and challenge the story of the United States.
